 {"id":"bd-2mj.1.4","title":"Usage forecasting and limit prediction","description":"Predict when users will hit rate limits based on historical usage patterns and current velocity.

## Background
Knowing when you'll hit a rate limit BEFORE it happens allows proactive account switching instead of reactive switching after hitting limits.

## Forecasting Models

### 1. Velocity-Based Prediction (Simple)
```
current_usage = 65%
velocity = 5% per hour (based on last 2 hours)
remaining = 35%
time_to_limit = remaining / velocity = 7 hours
```

### 2. Pattern-Based Prediction (Advanced)
- Use historical hourly patterns
- Weight recent data more heavily
- Account for day-of-week patterns
```
predicted_usage[t+1] = current_usage + weighted_avg(historical_deltas[same_hour])
```

### 3. Confidence Intervals
- Calculate standard deviation of predictions
- Provide pessimistic/optimistic bounds
```
time_to_limit_min = prediction - 2*stddev
time_to_limit_max = prediction + 2*stddev
```

## Implementation

### Forecasting Engine
```rust
pub struct UsageForecast {
    /// Predicted time until hitting limit
    pub time_to_limit: Option<Duration>,
    
    /// Confidence interval
    pub time_to_limit_min: Option<Duration>,
    pub time_to_limit_max: Option<Duration>,
    
    /// Prediction confidence (0.0 - 1.0)
    pub confidence: f64,
    
    /// Predicted usage at specific times
    pub predictions: Vec<(DateTime<Utc>, f64)>,
    
    /// Method used for prediction
    pub method: ForecastMethod,
}

pub enum ForecastMethod {
    Velocity,      // Simple linear extrapolation
    Historical,    // Pattern-based
    Hybrid,        // Combination
    Insufficient,  // Not enough data
}

pub fn forecast_usage(
    account: &Account,
    current: &UsageSnapshot,
    history: &[UsageSnapshot],
) -> UsageForecast { ... }
```

### Historical Data Requirements
- Minimum 24 hours of data for Velocity method
- Minimum 7 days for Pattern-based method
- More data = higher confidence

 {"id":"bd-2mj.1.5","title":"Anomaly detection for usage patterns","description":"Detect and alert on unusual usage patterns that deviate from historical norms.

## Background
Anomaly detection helps users identify:
- Unexpected usage spikes (possible runaway processes)
- Missing expected usage (account issues, auth problems)
- Cost anomalies (unexpected billing)
- Pattern changes (workflow changes)

## Types of Anomalies

### 1. Usage Spikes
- Usage increases significantly faster than normal
- Compare current velocity to historical velocity
- Alert threshold: 2+ standard deviations above mean

### 2. Usage Gaps
- Expected usage period with no activity
- Based on historical patterns (e.g., normally active 9am-5pm)
- Alert if no snapshots during expected active hours

### 3. Account Dormancy
- Account not used for extended period
- May indicate auth issues or forgotten account
- Alert threshold: Configurable (default 7 days)

### 4. Cost Anomalies
- Session costs significantly higher than normal
- Daily/weekly cost deviation from trend
- Alert threshold: 50%+ above moving average

### 5. Pattern Shifts
- Long-term changes in usage patterns
- Detected via rolling window comparison
- Informational alert (not necessarily bad)

## Detection Algorithms

### Z-Score Method (Simple)
```
z_score = (current_value - mean) / stddev
anomaly if abs(z_score) > 2.0
```

### IQR Method (Robust)
```
Q1 = 25th percentile
Q3 = 75th percentile
IQR = Q3 - Q1
lower_bound = Q1 - 1.5 * IQR
upper_bound = Q3 + 1.5 * IQR
anomaly if value < lower_bound or value > upper_bound
```

### Moving Average Deviation
```
ma = moving_average(last_7_days)
deviation = abs(current - ma) / ma
anomaly if deviation > 0.5 (50%)
```

## Schema Additions

```sql
CREATE TABLE anomalies (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  account_id TEXT REFERENCES accounts(id),
  detected_at TEXT NOT NULL,
  
  -- Anomaly classification
  anomaly_type TEXT NOT NULL,  -- 'spike', 'gap', 'dormant', 'cost', 'shift'
  severity TEXT NOT NULL,      -- 'info', 'warning', 'critical'
  
  -- Detection details
  expected_value REAL,
  actual_value REAL,
  deviation_percent REAL,
  
  -- Context
  description TEXT,
  
  -- Resolution
  acknowledged BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
  acknowledged_at TEXT,
  resolution_notes TEXT
);

CREATE INDEX idx_anomalies_time ON anomalies(detected_at);
CREATE INDEX idx_anomalies_account ON anomalies(account_id);
```

 {"id":"bd-2mj.1.6","title":"Account rotation analytics and optimization","description":"Analyze account rotation patterns and provide optimization recommendations for users with many accounts.

## Background
Users with 20+ accounts need to know:
- Which accounts are underutilized
- Which accounts are being over-relied upon
- Optimal rotation strategy
- Overall 'fleet health' metrics

## Metrics to Track

### Per-Account Metrics
- Total usage time (hours active)
- Total tokens processed
- Average utilization when active
- Number of switches to this account
- Time since last use
- Limit hits count

### Fleet-Wide Metrics
- Account balance score (even distribution)
- Total available capacity across all accounts
- Effective utilization rate
- Rotation frequency

## Fleet Health Score
```
balance_score = 1 - (stddev(usage_per_account) / mean(usage_per_account))
capacity_score = sum(remaining_percent) / (100 * account_count)
health_score = (balance_score + capacity_score) / 2
```

## Rotation Optimization Algorithm
```rust
pub fn recommend_next_account(
    accounts: &[AccountStatus],
    current: &AccountStatus,
) -> Option<AccountRecommendation> {
    // Score each account based on:
    // 1. Available capacity (higher = better)
    // 2. Time since last use (longer = better for balance)
    // 3. Historical performance (fewer auth issues = better)
    // 4. Usage pattern match (similar times of day = better)
    
    accounts.iter()
        .filter(|a| a.id != current.id)
        .filter(|a| a.remaining_percent > 20.0)  // At least 20% remaining
        .map(|a| (a, score_account(a)))
        .max_by(|a, b| a.1.partial_cmp(&b.1).unwrap())
        .map(|(a, score)| AccountRecommendation {
            account: a.clone(),
            score,
            reasons: generate_reasons(a, score),
        })
}
```

 {"id":"bd-2no","title":"Create theme system for consistent styling","description":"## Task Overview
Design and implement a theme system that provides consistent, customizable styling across all rich output components, with automatic terminal capability detection.

## Background

### Why a Theme System?

pub fn provider_style(name: &str, theme: &Theme) -> Style {
    let name_lower = name.to_lowercase();
    match name_lower.as_str() {
        s if s.contains("claude") || s.contains("anthropic") => theme.provider_claude.clone(),
        s if s.contains("openai") || s.contains("gpt") || s.contains("codex") => theme.provider_openai.clone(),
        s if s.contains("google") || s.contains("gemini") => theme.provider_google.clone(),
        s if s.contains("cursor") => theme.provider_cursor.clone(),
        s if s.contains("copilot") || s.contains("github") => theme.provider_copilot.clone(),
        _ => theme.provider_other.clone(),
    }
}

### Terminal Capability Detection
```rust
use tracing::{debug, instrument};

/// Detect terminal color capabilities
#[instrument(level = "debug")]
pub fn detect_color_depth() -> ColorDepth {
    // Check NO_COLOR first
    if std::env::var("NO_COLOR").is_ok() {
        debug!(reason = "NO_COLOR", "Color depth: NoColor");
        return ColorDepth::NoColor;
    }
    
    // Check COLORTERM for truecolor
    if let Ok(colorterm) = std::env::var("COLORTERM") {
        if colorterm == "truecolor" || colorterm == "24bit" {
            debug!(reason = "COLORTERM", colorterm = %colorterm, "Color depth: TrueColor");
            return ColorDepth::TrueColor;
        }
    }
    
    // Check TERM for 256 colors
    if let Ok(term) = std::env::var("TERM") {
        if term.contains("256color") {
            debug!(reason = "TERM", term = %term, "Color depth: Extended");
            return ColorDepth::Extended;
        }
        if term == "dumb" {
            debug!(reason = "TERM=dumb", "Color depth: NoColor");
            return ColorDepth::NoColor;
        }
    }
    
    // Default to basic colors
    debug!(reason = "default", "Color depth: Basic");
    ColorDepth::Basic
}

/// Check if terminal supports Unicode
pub fn has_unicode_support() -> bool {
    // Check LANG/LC_ALL for UTF-8
    let lang = std::env::var("LANG").unwrap_or_default();
    let lc_all = std::env::var("LC_ALL").unwrap_or_default();
    
    lang.contains("UTF-8") || lang.contains("utf8") ||
    lc_all.contains("UTF-8") || lc_all.contains("utf8")
}
```

        status_error: Style::new().foreground(Color::Red).bold(),
        
        box_style: BoxStyle::Heavy,
        color_depth: detect_color_depth(),
    }
}

pub fn ascii_theme() -> Theme {
    Theme {
        box_style: BoxStyle::Ascii,
        ..default_theme()
    }
}

### Theme Selection with Priority

```rust
#[instrument(level = "debug", skip(args))]
pub fn get_theme(args: &Args) -> Theme {
    // Priority 1: CLI flag (--theme)
    if let Some(name) = &args.theme {
        debug!(source = "cli_flag", theme = %name, "Theme selected via CLI");
        return theme_by_name(name);
    }
    
    // Priority 2: Environment variable
    if let Ok(name) = std::env::var("CAUT_THEME") {
        debug!(source = "env_var", theme = %name, "Theme selected via CAUT_THEME");
        return theme_by_name(&name);
    }
    
    // Priority 3: Auto-detect based on capabilities
    if !has_unicode_support() {
        debug!(source = "auto_detect", reason = "no_unicode", "Using ASCII theme");
        return ascii_theme();
    }
    
    // Priority 4: Default
    debug!(source = "default", "Using default theme");
    default_theme()
}

pub fn theme_by_name(name: &str) -> Theme {
    match name.to_lowercase().as_str() {
        "default" => default_theme(),
        "minimal" | "min" => minimal_theme(),
        "high-contrast" | "highcontrast" | "hc" => high_contrast_theme(),
        "ascii" | "plain" => ascii_theme(),
        unknown => {
            tracing::warn!(theme = %unknown, "Unknown theme, using default");
            default_theme()
        }
    }
}
```

## Concept
Allow users to type natural queries and get meaningful responses.

## Example Queries

### Usage Questions
- How much have I used this week?
- Which Claude account has the most remaining?
- Am I going to hit my limit today?

### Analytical Questions  
- What are my peak usage hours?
- Which account should I switch to?
- Why is my usage higher than usual?

### Action Requests
- Switch to my freshest account
- Show me accounts I havent used recently
- Give me a summary of this month

## Implementation Options

### 1. Pattern Matching (Simple)
- Regex-based intent detection
- Keyword extraction
- Good enough for common queries

### 2. Local LLM (Advanced)
- Use small local model (Phi, Llama-small)
- Better understanding of complex queries
- Works offline

### 3. API-Based (Optional)
- Use Claude/GPT for query understanding
- Best accuracy
- Requires API access

## CLI Integration

caut ask

Today youve used 2.3M tokens across 3 accounts:
  jeff@example.com: 1.2M (52%)
  jeff141421@: 890K (39%)
  jeff173205@: 210K (9%)

> Which account should I use next?
Recommended: jeff173205@gmail.com
Reason: 91% remaining, hasnt been used in 4 hours

### Single Query Mode  
caut ask How much remaining on my primary Claude?

 {"id":"bd-aop","title":"Implement rich summary table for usage command","description":"## Task Overview
Create a comprehensive summary table that displays all provider usage data in a single, well-formatted table with totals.

## Desired State
```
┌──────────────────────── Usage Summary ────────────────────────┐
│ Provider       │ Requests │     Tokens │     Cost │   Status  │
├────────────────┼──────────┼────────────┼──────────┼───────────┤
│ Claude         │    1,234 │      5.67M │   $45.67 │     ✓     │
│ OpenAI         │      567 │      2.35M │   $23.45 │     ✓     │
├────────────────┼──────────┼────────────┼──────────┼───────────┤
│ TOTAL          │    1,801 │      8.02M │   $69.12 │  2/2 OK   │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Implementation

### Table Creation
```rust
fn create_summary_table(providers: &[ProviderUsage], theme: &Theme) -> Table {
    let mut table = Table::new()
        .title("Usage Summary")
        .box_style(theme.box_style.clone());
    // Add columns, rows, totals...
}
```

### Formatting Functions
- format_number(): 1234567 -> "1,234,567"
- format_tokens_compact(): 5678901 -> "5.67M"
- format_cost(): 45.678 -> "$45.68"
- format_status(): Success -> "✓"

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    
    #[test]
    fn test_create_summary_table_empty() {
        let table = create_summary_table(&[], &default_theme());
        let out = table.render().to_string();
        assert!(out.contains("Usage Summary") || out.contains("No data"));
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_create_summary_table_single() {
        let table = create_summary_table(&[sample_claude()], &default_theme());
        let out = table.render().to_string();
        assert!(out.contains("Claude"));
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_create_summary_table_multiple() {
        let providers = vec![sample_claude(), sample_openai(), sample_gemini()];
        let table = create_summary_table(&providers, &default_theme());
        let out = table.render().to_string();
        assert!(out.contains("Claude") && out.contains("OpenAI") && out.contains("Gemini"));
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_create_summary_table_has_totals() {
        let table = create_summary_table(&sample_all(), &default_theme());
        let out = table.render().to_string();
        assert!(out.contains("TOTAL") || out.contains("Total"));
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_create_summary_table_plain_no_ansi() {
        let plain = create_summary_table_plain(&sample_all(), &default_theme());
        assert!(!plain.contains("\x1b["));
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_format_number() {
        assert_eq!(format_number(1234567), "1,234,567");
        assert_eq!(format_number(0), "0");
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_format_tokens_compact() {
        assert_eq!(format_tokens_compact(5_678_901), "5.68M");
        assert_eq!(format_tokens_compact(1_234), "1.23K");
        assert_eq!(format_tokens_compact(123), "123");
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_format_cost() {
        assert_eq!(format_cost(45.678), "$45.68");
        assert_eq!(format_cost(0.0), "-");
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_format_status() {
        assert_eq!(format_status(&FetchStatus::Success), "✓");
        assert_eq!(format_status(&FetchStatus::Failed), "✗");
    }
}
```

 {"id":"bd-yfrc","title":"Enhance help output with rich formatting","description":"## Task Overview
Enhance the --help output and help command with rich formatting including styled sections, examples, and improved readability.

## Background & Reasoning

### Current State
Help output uses default clap formatting which is functional but plain.

### Desired State
Rich, well-organized help output:
```
╭─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│                                                             │
│  caut - Coding Agent Usage Tracker                          │
│                                                             │
│  Track and display usage metrics from 16+ LLM providers     │
│                                                             │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

Usage: caut [COMMAND] [OPTIONS]

Commands:
  usage    Fetch and display usage data from all configured providers
  cost     Show cost breakdown and analysis
  doctor   Run diagnostic checks on configuration and connectivity
  history  Display historical usage data and trends
  config   Manage configuration settings
  help     Print this help message or help for a subcommand

Options:
  -r, --robot      Output in robot-friendly format (JSON/Markdown)
  -p, --provider   Fetch from specific provider only
  -v, --verbose    Enable verbose output
  -h, --help       Print help information
  -V, --version    Print version information

Examples:
  caut usage              # Fetch usage from all providers
  caut usage -p claude    # Fetch only Claude usage
  caut cost --period 30d  # Show cost for last 30 days
  caut doctor             # Run diagnostic checks

Environment Variables:
  ANTHROPIC_API_KEY   Claude/Anthropic API key
  OPENAI_API_KEY      OpenAI/Codex API key
  GOOGLE_API_KEY      Google/Gemini API key
  ...

Documentation: https://github.com/username/caut
```

### Why Rich Help?
- **Better UX**: Help is easier to read and navigate
- **Examples**: Show practical usage examples
- **Environment vars**: Document required configuration
- **Professional**: Polished first impression

## Implementation Details

### RichHelp Component

```rust
pub struct RichHelp<'a> {
    command: &'a Command,
    theme: &'a Theme,
}

impl RichHelp<'_> {
    pub fn render(&self) -> String {
        let mut output = String::new();
        
        // Header panel
        output.push_str(&self.render_header());
        output.push_str("\n\n");
        
        // Usage section
        output.push_str(&self.render_usage());
        output.push_str("\n\n");
        
        // Commands section
        if self.command.has_subcommands() {
            output.push_str(&self.render_commands());
            output.push_str("\n\n");
        }
        
        // Options section
        output.push_str(&self.render_options());
        output.push_str("\n\n");
        
        // Examples section
        output.push_str(&self.render_examples());
        output.push_str("\n\n");
        
        // Environment variables
        output.push_str(&self.render_env_vars());
        output.push_str("\n\n");
        
        // Footer
        output.push_str(&self.render_footer());
        
        output
    }
    
    fn render_header(&self) -> String {
        let panel = Panel::from_text(&format!(
            "{}\n\n{}",
            self.theme.primary.render("caut - Coding Agent Usage Tracker"),
            "Track and display usage metrics from 16+ LLM providers"
        ))
        .box_style(self.theme.box_style.clone())
        .border_style(self.theme.panel_border.clone());
        
        panel.render_to_string()
    }
    
    fn render_commands(&self) -> String {
        let mut lines = vec![self.theme.secondary.render("Commands:")];
        
        let commands = vec![
            ("usage", "Fetch and display usage data from all providers"),
            ("cost", "Show cost breakdown and analysis"),
            ("doctor", "Run diagnostic checks"),
            ("history", "Display historical usage data"),
            ("config", "Manage configuration settings"),
            ("help", "Print help for a command"),
        ];
        
        for (name, desc) in commands {
            lines.push(format!(
                "  {:<10} {}",
                self.theme.primary.render(name),
                desc
            ));
        }
        
        lines.join("\n")
    }
    
    fn render_examples(&self) -> String {
        let mut lines = vec![self.theme.secondary.render("Examples:")];
        
        let examples = vec![
            ("caut usage", "Fetch usage from all providers"),
            ("caut usage -p claude", "Fetch only Claude usage"),
            ("caut cost --period 30d", "Show cost for last 30 days"),
            ("caut doctor", "Run diagnostic checks"),
        ];
        
        for (cmd, comment) in examples {
            lines.push(format!(
                "  {}  {}  {}",
                self.theme.primary.render(cmd),
                self.theme.muted.render("#"),
                self.theme.muted.render(comment)
            ));
        }
        
        lines.join("\n")
    }
    
    fn render_env_vars(&self) -> String {
        let mut lines = vec![self.theme.secondary.render("Environment Variables:")];
        
        let vars = vec![
            ("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", "Claude/Anthropic API key"),
            ("OPENAI_API_KEY", "OpenAI/Codex API key"),
            ("GOOGLE_API_KEY", "Google/Gemini API key"),
        ];
        
        for (name, desc) in vars {
            lines.push(format!(
                "  {:<20} {}",
                self.theme.primary.render(name),
                desc
            ));
        }
        
        lines.push(self.theme.muted.render("  ... and more. Run 'caut help providers' for full list."));
        
        lines.join("\n")
    }
    
    pub fn render_plain(&self) -> String {
        // Fall back to clap's default help
        self.command.render_help().to_string()
    }
}
```
